name: Backchannel Diplomacy
summary: Call privately and find out whether there is still a way to stop this before it gets worse.

delayedEffects.description: Backchannel trust dividends materialize.

variants.label: Quiet Probe
variants.summary: Send a quiet message through trusted intermediaries and ask what would make the pressure stop.

delayedEffects.description: Quiet channel slows the rush into formal coercion.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It buys a sliver of time, but it also increases the risk that a false signal will be mistaken for a real off-ramp.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as time-buying and ambiguity-preserving.

variants.label: Firm Channel
variants.summary: Use the private channel, but make the conditions and consequences unmistakable.

delayedEffects.description: Private conditions create a clearer, but riskier, diplomatic line.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: The signal is clearer and riskier; if Beijing rejects it, the next step becomes harder to keep reversible.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as private diplomacy with sharper edges.

name: Public Strategic Address
summary: Go on camera, tell the public what is happening, and make the line clear.

variants.label: Calibrated Address
variants.summary: Tell the public and allies where the line is without promising the next military step.

delayedEffects.description: A disciplined public line steadies allied messaging.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It aims to calm allies and markets without yet turning the crisis into a public test of absolute resolve.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as message discipline first, escalation second.

variants.label: Hard Red Line
variants.summary: Make a hard public warning and force Beijing to decide whether to back down or test it.

delayedEffects.description: A hard public line narrows room for quiet retreat.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It sharpens the stakes immediately and dares the counterpart to either absorb the line or push against it.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as overt deterrence with reputational commitment.

name: Targeted Sanctions
summary: Freeze and punish the firms and people helping keep the pressure campaign alive.

delayedEffects.description: Compliance pressure accumulates as targeted sanctions propagate.

variants.label: Signaling Tranche
variants.summary: Start narrow: hit the clearest targets without shocking the whole financial system.

delayedEffects.description: Targeted compliance pressure begins to accumulate.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It tells markets and allies that costs are coming, but tries to keep the first package survivable.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as controlled cost imposition.

variants.label: Enforcement Wave
variants.summary: Move harder so the companies behind the pressure feel pain fast.

delayedEffects.description: A stronger enforcement wave widens compliance and market stress.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It imposes real pain faster, but it also increases the odds that shipping, financing, and political blowback outrun the coalition’s control.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as sharper pressure with faster spillover risk.

name: Broad Sanctions
summary: Launch the big sanctions package and accept that markets and supply chains will feel it immediately.

delayedEffects.description: Sanctions lag drives domestic cost and retaliatory pressure.

variants.label: Prepared Package
variants.summary: Show that the full package is ready, but phase it in to avoid a market break tonight.

delayedEffects.description: Markets begin pricing the larger sanctions package before all measures are live.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It points the crisis toward the financial channel quickly without pulling every lever at once.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as pre-positioned macro pressure.

variants.label: Maximal Package
variants.summary: Push the full package now and accept the immediate hit to shipping, banks, and chip supply.

delayedEffects.description: The maximal sanctions package triggers broader financial and shipping repricing.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It makes the line unmistakable, but it also risks turning the financial system into a second battlefield.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as systemic cost imposition with little commercial cushioning.

name: Resource Stockpiling
summary: Build fuel, medical, chip, and shipping reserves before the crisis reaches shelves and gas stations.

delayedEffects.description: Stockpiles absorb follow-on supply volatility.

variants.label: Preposition Reserves
variants.summary: Build reserves quietly so the country has more time without setting off panic buying.

delayedEffects.description: Reserve buffers improve the coalition’s political and logistical endurance.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It buys breathing room for fuel, spares, and coalition nerves, but if the move leaks it can tell the market the room expects something worse very soon.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as quiet buffer-building.

variants.label: Emergency Buffer
variants.summary: Move fast and visibly to build reserves before the lane gets worse.

delayedEffects.description: Emergency reserve activity supports endurance but advertises stress to the market.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It hardens the buffer fast, but the urgency itself can make buyers, insurers, and political aides act as if the break has already begun.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as visible resilience at the cost of signaling stress.

name: Cyber Intrusion
summary: Get inside the other side's systems to see what they are preparing, without breaking anything.

delayedEffects.description: Intercepted insight improves response readiness.

variants.label: Forensic Pull
variants.summary: Use cyber access to find who is directing the pressure and what they may do next.

delayedEffects.description: Forensic cyber access improves attribution and reduces the rival’s information edge.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It is designed to steal clarity back from the fog rather than add another visible shock.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as hidden collection and attribution.

variants.label: Deep Mapping
variants.summary: Push deeper to map the whole setup before it produces another shock.

delayedEffects.description: Deeper cyber mapping exposes more of the adversary’s setup but carries higher discovery risk.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It reaches for deeper clarity faster, but the discovery risk rises with it.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as aggressive but still deniable collection.

name: Cyber Disruption
summary: Interrupt the systems helping the other side move, cue, and coordinate pressure.

variants.label: Reversible Nuisance
variants.summary: Create a limited cyber disruption that slows them down without making cyber war the headline.

delayedEffects.description: Reversible cyber friction disrupts the coercive setup without fully detonating the response loop.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It creates pain and delay, but keeps a thin layer of reversibility in the response.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as limited cyber friction rather than full retaliation.

variants.label: Hard Disruption
variants.summary: Hit hard enough to interrupt the next move, knowing retaliation becomes more likely.

delayedEffects.description: A harder cyber hit interrupts the next move but makes retaliation and spillover more likely.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It can break the immediate tempo, but the response will be read as a much clearer crossing into offensive action.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as offensive cyber escalation.

name: Covert Sabotage
summary: Quietly help slow the other side's logistics while keeping U.S. fingerprints off the move.

variants.label: Deniable Cutout
variants.summary: Use cutouts to slow the pressure network without owning the move in public.

delayedEffects.description: Covert disruption introduces friction while preserving a degree of deniability.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It adds hidden friction, but if it leaks, the crisis can become harder to narrate and harder to control.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as deniable leverage.

variants.label: High-Tempo Cutout
variants.summary: Move faster in the shadows so the next pressure move arrives late or broken.

delayedEffects.description: Higher-tempo covert pressure creates more friction but also more exposure and political blowback risk.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It reaches for sharper hidden leverage at the cost of a much rougher political landing if attribution catches up.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as sharper deniable disruption.

name: Military Posture Increase
summary: Move more forces into position and make clear the United States is ready to take risk.

delayedEffects.description: Sustained deployment costs begin to bite.

variants.label: Shadow Surge
variants.summary: Move more capability into place than you announce, so the room gets stronger before the headlines catch up.

delayedEffects.description: Additional forces arrive, but the cost to future surge slack becomes harder to hide.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It improves deterrence while trying not to trigger the loudest possible reaction immediately.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as real posture without maximal publicity.

variants.label: Broadcast Deterrence
variants.summary: Make the military move visible enough that everyone knows the coalition is willing to take risk.

delayedEffects.description: A visible deterrent signal clarifies resolve but also raises the chance of sharper repricing and confrontation.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It turns deterrence into public theater, which can restore clarity or force the other side to answer in kind.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as visible deterrence with reputational commitment.

name: Military Posture Decrease
summary: Pull back the most visible military pressure and see if the other side will step down too.

variants.label: Quiet Reposition
variants.summary: Lower the visible edge while keeping enough hidden capability to avoid looking absent.

delayedEffects.description: A quieter posture eases some immediate stress, though it may also invite testing.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It preserves future slack and lowers the immediate heat, but only if the counterpart does not treat it as an invitation.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as reversible de-intensification.

variants.label: Public Decompression
variants.summary: Announce a visible pullback and invite Beijing to match it.

delayedEffects.description: The visible decompression lowers heat but increases the risk the other side treats the pause as room to press.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It maximizes restraint as a signal, but that signal can land as relief or weakness depending on what the other side wanted all along.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as overt de-escalation.

name: Intelligence Surge
summary: Shift more eyes and analysts onto the crisis so the room is not guessing through the next move.

delayedEffects.description: Better attribution improves escalation management.

variants.label: Fusion Cell
variants.summary: Pull allied intelligence into one cell and cut through the noise before the next move lands.

delayedEffects.description: The surge in collection improves attribution and reduces surprise in the next window.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It is a clarity move first and a signaling move second, meant to stop the crisis from being decided by the dirtiest picture.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as clarity and warning-time recovery.

variants.label: Allied Attribution Cell
variants.summary: Make the evidence effort visibly allied so any later accusation rests on more than Washington's word.

delayedEffects.description: Shared attribution improves coalition discipline in the next response window.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It turns intelligence into coalition glue, which can be slower than a unilateral move but more stabilizing if the branch worsens.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as alliance-backed clarity.

name: Offer Limited Concession
summary: Offer a narrow compromise and demand proof that the pressure stops.

delayedEffects.description: Partial détente eases external risk premium.

variants.label: Technical Pause
variants.summary: Offer a small reversible pause and see whether Beijing wants a way out or just a better story.

delayedEffects.description: A narrow concession gives markets and carriers a brief reason to pause the worst repricing.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It is an offer of process, not surrender, but it can still be read as the first sign that pressure works.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as reversible technical relief.

variants.label: Public Offramp
variants.summary: Put a public way out on the table and make Beijing choose whether to reject it in front of everyone.

delayedEffects.description: A visible offramp can stabilize allies and markets if the counterpart does not reject it outright.

variants.narrativeEmphasis: It aims to flip the burden of escalation onto Beijing, but if Beijing shrugs it off, the coalition may look like it blinked first.
variants.advisorFraming: Framed as public diplomatic challenge.

name: Rival Public Threat
summary: Issue explicit warning with deadline language.

name: Rival Covert Probe
summary: Deniable cyber and HUMINT probing against critical systems.

name: Rival Sanction Retaliation
summary: Counter-sanctions on strategic imports and finance channels.

delayedEffects.description: Retaliatory restrictions continue to degrade domestic confidence.

name: Rival Military Drill
summary: Large-scale readiness drill near contested corridor.

name: Rival De-Escalation Signal
summary: Signal willingness for managed pause and verification.

name: Rival Energy Coercion
summary: Constrict energy flows to pressure domestic resilience.

delayedEffects.description: Energy shock amplifies domestic anxiety.

name: Rival Disinformation Campaign
summary: Amplify narratives to fracture domestic trust and alliance cohesion.

name: Rival Proxy Incident
summary: A deniable proxy engages in limited kinetic provocation.

name: Rival Backchannel Reply
summary: Respond through private channels with conditional terms.

delayedEffects.description: Backchannel procedural talks reduce immediate pressure.
